title: goodenia drummondii subsp. megaphylla
intro: Goodenia drummondii subsp. megaphylla is a sub-species of the Goodenia drummondii plant, which is a type of flowering plant native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and fight off infections. The health benefits of Goodenia drummondii subsp. megaphylla include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
